description:
You are a FinOps analyst.  
Inside the working directory /home/user/cloud_costs there is an existing daily spend file named costs.csv.  
The file has a header row followed by comma-separated records in the form

service,date,cost

where  
• service is a plain string with no embedded commas,  
• date is in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD,  
• cost is a decimal number that always uses a dot as the decimal separator.

Goal  
1. Read /home/user/cloud_costs/costs.csv.  
2. Calculate, **for the month of September 2023 only (2023-09-01 through 2023-09-30 inclusive)**, the total spend per unique service and the overall total spend across all services.  
3. Produce two artefacts in /home/user/cloud_costs:

   a) monthly_summary.json – UTF-8 encoded, exact structure:

   {
     "month": "2023-09",
     "services": [
       {"name": "<service-A>", "total": <A_total_as_number_with_two_decimals>},
       {"name": "<service-B>", "total": <B_total_as_number_with_two_decimals>},
       …
     ],
     "overall_total": <overall_total_as_number_with_two_decimals>
   }

   • The objects inside "services" must appear in **lexicographical order by the name value**.  
   • Every numeric value must be rendered with exactly two digits after the decimal point (e.g. 12.00).  
   • Do not include any additional keys or metadata.

   b) summary.log – plain text, one line per service followed by one overall line, all terminated with Unix LF:

   <service-A>,<A_total_with_two_decimals>
   <service-B>,<B_total_with_two_decimals>
   …
   OVERALL,<overall_total_with_two_decimals>

   • The service lines must be in the same lexicographical order used in monthly_summary.json.  
   • The final OVERALL line must come immediately after the last service line (no blank lines).

Constraints  
• You can accomplish this with a small number of shell commands of your choice (awk, sort, jq, python -c, etc.).  
• Do not modify the original costs.csv.  
• The script/commands you write may assume that costs.csv already exists and is readable by the current user.  
• When finished, both /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly_summary.json and /home/user/cloud_costs/summary.log must exist exactly as specified so that automated grading can validate them.
truth:
Initial files/directories present before the agent starts:

1. Directory: /home/user/cloud_costs  
   Permissions: 755

2. File: /home/user/cloud_costs/costs.csv  
   Permissions: 644  
   Content (including header line):

service,date,cost
EC2,2023-09-01,10.50
EC2,2023-09-15,20.00
EC2,2023-08-20,5.00
RDS,2023-09-05,30.00
RDS,2023-09-20,40.00
S3,2023-09-10,50.11
S3,2023-09-11,52.11

Files that must exist after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/cloud_costs/monthly_summary.json  
   Permissions: 644  
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including newlines and spaces):

{
  "month": "2023-09",
  "services": [
    {"name": "EC2", "total": 30.50},
    {"name": "RDS", "total": 70.00},
    {"name": "S3", "total": 102.22}
  ],
  "overall_total": 202.72
}

2. /home/user/cloud_costs/summary.log  
   Permissions: 644  
   Exact content (each line terminated by a single LF):

EC2,30.50
RDS,70.00
S3,102.22
OVERALL,202.72

Automated tests will:

• Verify that both files exist.  
• Check that the JSON is valid, matches the exact structure, has services in the correct order, and numeric values rendered with two decimal digits.  
• Check that summary.log lines match exactly, in order, with no extra whitespace or blank lines.  
• Ensure that costs.csv remains unaltered.
